context: One of the most sacred mantras in Hinduism, from the Rigveda (3.62.10). Attributed to Rishi Vishvamitra, it addresses the solar deity Savitri and is a prayer for intellectual and spiritual illumination. Often called the "Mother of the Vedas." Traditionally recited at dawn, noon, and dusk during sandhyavandana.

verses.hindi:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः

                        तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं

                        भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि

                        धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah
Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat
verses.translation:
Om, the sacred sound, across the earth, atmosphere, and heavens,
we meditate upon the most adorable
radiance of the Divine Creator (Savitri).
May that divine light illuminate and inspire our intellects.
